data.title: Alaska Air warns of Q1 profit hit from high fuel costs, Puerto Vallarta unrest, and Hawaii storms
data.description:
Alaska Air Group (ALK) provided a guidance update after accounting for fuel costs that have increased materially due to sharply higher crude and refining prices.

Despite what has become another challenging first-quarter operating environment in 2026, the airline company said it continues to execute well on its strategy and areas of the business within its control, with encouraging revenue trends heading into the peak travel season. Unit revenue is tracking in line with prior expectations and capacity toward the high end of the previously guided range, up ~2%.

However, the clear underlying demand strength that began in Q4 of 2025 and accelerated into the new year was noted to have recently been challenged by several external events. A demand pullback in Mexico due to unrest in Puerto Vallarta as well as severe rainstorms and historic flooding in Hawaii were major factors since they combine to represent ~30% of ALK's capacity. Impacts are being seen in both March and April, including during the peak West Coast spring break travel periods.

Revenue trends across the rest of the network heading into Q2 were noted to be encouraging. Managed corporate demand remains a standout, with forward bookings over the next 90 days up more than 25% year over year. Held second quarter yields and load factors are also up year over year, with significant strength in May and June. With 55% of the quarter’s revenue still to come, Alaska Air (ALK) said it is well positioned for peak travel periods during its seasonally strongest quarter.

In terms of fuel costs, Alaska Air (ALK) said its lowest-cost source of fuel typically comes from Singapore, which represents approximately 20% of its fuel supply. Those refining margins have surged ~400% since early February, from an average of ~$0.45 to ~$2.25 per gallon. As a result, the economic fuel price is now expected to average $2.90 to $3.00 per gallon, representing an incremental EPS headwind of at least ($0.70). Due to the impacts from fuel, Puerto Vallarta, and Hawaii storms, ALK's Q1 adjusted loss per share is now expected to be $2.00 to $1.50 vs. the -$0.98 consensus.

Shares of Alaska Air (ALK) traded 1.1% lower in premarket action on Monday.

data.title: Trump says U.S. in talks with 'new, responsible regime' in Iran to end military operations
data.description:
President Donald Trump said that the U.S. is in talks with a "new and more reasonable regime" of Iran to end their military operations in the Middle Eastern country. 

Trump, however, warned that if a deal is not reached shortly and if the Strait of Hormuz is not immediately open, then the U.S. will blow up Iran's electric generating plants, oil wells, and Kharg Island.

"This will be in retribution for our many soldiers, and others, that Iran has butchered and killed over the old regime's 47-year 'reign of terror,'" Trump wrote on Truth Social.  

Trump, on Thursday, had extended a pause on U.S. strikes targeting Iran's energy infrastructure, pushing the deadline to April 6 as diplomatic efforts continue between Washington and Tehran.

The move had marked the second time he had extended the deadline since first warning on March 21 that Iran's power infrastructure could be targeted unless Tehran swiftly reopened the strait to commercial shipping.

The president had originally given Iran 48 hours to comply and then last week—just hours before that window closed—announced a five-day delay, pointing to what he described as constructive discussions. Still, Tehran has denied it is in direct talks with the U.S.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Avis Budget shares under pressure on impending stock sale
data.description:
• Shares of Avis Budget Group (CAR) are down more than 7% into Monday’s open as the car rental company disclosed plans to sell 5M shares after the close on Friday.
  • The sale will be conducted “from time to time” and will be made at the market. Agents who facilitate any sale will be compensated at 2% of the gross sale price.
  • The proceeds of the sale will be used for general corporate purposes.

data.title: OpenAI's ChatGPT app store initiative sees sluggish start: report
data.description:
Six months since OpenAI's (OPENAI) initiative to integrate mini apps within ChatGPT was announced, the progress has been sluggish, Bloomberg News reported, citing interviews with app makers.

There are now over 300 app integrations available, but they are hidden away, and the functionality has been limited by the partner companies, which are hesitant to hand over customer relationships and payments to OpenAI. Developers have also complained regarding a tedious app-approval process, a buggy coding system, and a lack of usage data, the report added.

OpenAI did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

The ChatGPT app push has the potential to escalate increasing competition with Apple (AAPL), steering consumers toward a new ecosystem for apps and services outside the App Store. Apple moved to rein that in November 2025, introducing a “mini apps” policy requiring so-called super apps to hand over a 15% cut of in-app purchases, the report noted.

StubHub and Booking (BKNG) do not yet see ChatGPT apps as a big marketing driver. "It’s easier to discover listings on Booking.com,” Booking CEO Glenn Fogel said in an interview, noting that referral traffic from ChatGPT is still "small." He added that the company still “spends an enormous amount” on advertising with Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) relative to what it spends on OpenAI’s chatbot.

OpenAI said its app platform is central to its product strategy. "We're still early in building this out, and we recognize there are areas where the developer experience needs to improve," a spokesperson said, adding that the company is committed to making the platform “more reliable, more predictable and easier to build on over time."

Several initial partners, including Figma (FIG), Expedia (EXPE) and Target (TGT), saw their stocks soar after the initial announcement, as the tie-up offered a new way to acquire users in the age of AI rather than be disrupted by it, according to the report.

DoorDash (DASH) has also seen that no single collaboration integration, whether it is the order button on ChatGPT, Google, or Yelp, "monopolizes customer attention," as per a company spokesperson, the report noted.

Few apps offer a checkout option that doesn’t force users to leave OpenAI’s chatbot. In the case of Uber, users must begin the experience with "@Uber" before telling the chatbot their pickup and dropoff points to see an estimated fare range. Riders can only complete the booking on Uber, in which the whole process is faster, the report added.

Ticket reseller StubHub allows ChatGPT users to search for concerts, events, and seat availability based on preferences such as budget or viewing angles. However, it also requires them to visit the website to complete their purchase or even just to zoom in on a seating map.

StubHub President and Chief Product Officer Nayaab Islam said that customers are not yet using chatbots as a “primary way” to buy things and cited consumer apprehension about sharing credit card details with AI assistants, the report added.

However, he noted that it would be "imprudent" to not participate in chatbot apps given the billions of users they have.

Developers have also found the app development process frustrating, the report noted.

Hanh Nguyen, CEO of Fractal, a startup that makes tools for non-coders to build chatbot apps, said that getting an app published to the app store has been a lengthy ordeal for some developers. She said some rejections have included "false signals" flagged by OpenAI's AI-assisted review system, which required intervention from the company’s human support staff to resolve, the report added.

OpenAI said it has been accelerating the pace on approvals, citing both a streamlined process and developers addressing previously identified issues, according to the report.

Elliot Garreffa, a co-founder of a third-party platform to track ChatGPT apps and test their performance, said some developers run into problems even after their apps are accepted. Because OpenAI defines chatbot prompts as private data, the programmers have found they receive “very limited” analytics on their app's performance, leaving them "running quite blind" regarding user engagement, Garreffa added.

data.title: Air Canada CEO Michael Rousseau to retire
data.description:
• Air Canada (ACDVF) (AC:CA) said CEO Michael Rousseau will retire by the end of the third quarter of 2026 after nearly two decades with the company.
  • Rousseau will remain CEO and stay on the board until his retirement. The company added that a comprehensive process is underway to identify the next leader.

data.title: ProCap Financial stockholders approve acquisition of AI agent lab CFO Silvia
data.description:
• ProCap Financial (BRR) said shareholders approved all proposals at its annual meeting, including issuing shares to acquire finance-focused AI agent lab CFO Silvia.
  • The transaction is expected to close in early April 2026, subject to customary closing conditions. Upon closing, ProCap Financial will become the first publicly traded AI agent lab
  • The combined company will have more than $30B in assets on the CFO Silvia platform.

data.title: Bicara Therapeutics GAAP EPS of -$0.68
data.description:
• Bicara Therapeutics press release (BCAX): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.68.
  • Cash, Cash Equivalents and Marketable Securities: As of December 31, 2025, Bicara had cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities of $414.8 million, compared to $489.7 million in cash and cash equivalents as of December 31, 2024. An additional $161.8 million in net proceeds was raised via an oversubscribed public offering in the first quarter of 2026.
  • Research and Development Expenses: Research and development expenses were $33.0 million for the fourth quarter of 2025 as compared to $19.9 million for the fourth quarter of 2024, and $125.1 million for the full year 2025 as compared to $63.6 million for the full year 2024. The increase was primarily due to costs associated with ongoing pivotal Phase 2/3 clinical trial, FORTIFI-HN01, as well as the Company’s ongoing Phase 1/1b dose expansion cohorts, and an increase in personnel costs.

data.title: Royalty Pharma in $500M R&D funding collaboration with J&J
data.description:
• Royalty Pharma (RPRX) is partnering with Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) in a $500M R&D funding deal over 2026–2027 to support the development of a new autoimmune treatment.
  • JNJ‑4804 is a novel co‑antibody therapy that blocks the complementary interleukin‑23 (IL‑23) and tumor necrosis factor (TNF) pathways, delivering synergistic effects on the pathogenesis of chronic immune‑mediated diseases.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Oil surges as Brent nears record monthly gain following Houthi attacks on Israel
data.description:
Oil prices continued to climb on Monday, with Brent (CO1:COM) nearing a record monthly gain, after weekend attacks by Yemen’s Houthis on Israel intensified the broader Middle East conflict involving the U.S., Israel, and Iran.

Brent crude futures (CO1:COM) jumped 2.4% to $115.26 a barrel after settling 4.2% ‌higher on Friday. The benchmark was headed for a ~60% monthly gain. U.S. West Texas Intermediate (CL1:COM) was up 1.8% at $101.45 after a 5.5% gain in the previous session, up 51% this month.

Tight physical market conditions are also evident in the forward curve, with Brent’s prompt spread in deep backwardation, ING analysts said. "The front‑month contract traded at a premium of more than $7/bbl on Monday, compared with a largely flat structure before the conflict, underscoring acute near‑term supply concerns."

Elsewhere, positioning data shows speculative interest remains elevated despite some trimming. Speculators reduced their net long in ICE Brent by 21,579 lots to 407,125 lots as of last Tuesday, driven largely by a reduction in gross longs, while positioning changes in NYMEX WTI were modest. Ongoing geopolitical uncertainty and supply risks have continued to underpin risk‑on positioning in the oil market so far this year, the analysts said.

European natural gas prices also moved sharply higher, rising as much as 3.4%, as the escalation in the Middle East reinforced concerns over LNG availability ahead of the summer storage refill season.

The Israeli military said on Monday that it was attacking the Iranian government's infrastructure ⁠throughout Tehran.

data.title: Veradermics reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Veradermics, Incorporated press release (MANE): Q4 Cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities totaled $141.9 million as of December 31, 2025. 
  • Net loss was $21.8 million for the fourth quarter of 2025, compared to a net loss of $5.7 million for the fourth quarter of 2024.
  • Veradermics expects its current cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities to support its current operating plans into 2029.
